_comment: Codex-native hooks for the decisions contract (docs 21/25). DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH
ONLY: Codex PreToolUse cannot hard-block (it supports systemMessage only and
continues after a failed hook), so nothing here is the enforcement boundary —
the authoritative block is the agent-neutral CLI validate gate + CI required
checks. These hooks inject the contract pointer at SessionStart and surface the
relevant rows after a fleet/CI config edit, for context/audit.
Scripts resolve the repo root via `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` (see each
script) — no CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT — so Claude and Codex invoke the SAME scripts
and the SAME neutral checker (tools/scripts/decisions_contract.py). Both hooks
are advisory, exit 0, and no-op for a checkout without the contract file, so a
